original: Pope-elect Stephen is selected to succeed Zacharias as Catholic Pope only to die of a stroke two days later
18th recorded perihelion passage of Halley's Comet
Jocelin, abbot of Melrose, is elected bishop of Glasgow
First dated edition of Maimonides' "Mishneh Torah"  is published, a code of Jewish religious law
Aragonese legal code formally recognised
Treaty of Longjumeau: French huguenots go on strike
Friesland joins Union of Utrecht
English Separatist Puritans John Greenwood and Henry Barrowe tried and sentenced to death on the charge of devising and circulating seditious books
French troops occupy Pinerolo Piedmont
France & England form alliance against Spain; England gets Dunkirk
James Francis Edward Stuart, Prince of Wales, attempts to land at Firth of Forth, Scotland, but is turned away by the British Royal Navy
Canada's first newspaper "Halifax Gazette" published in Halifax by John Bushell
Novel "Les Liaisons dangereuses" (Dangerous Liaisons) by Pierre Choderlos de Laclos is published by Durand Neveu
American inventor Josiah Pierson patents a "cold-header" (rivet) machine
Battle and fall of city of Kalamata, Greek War of Independence
First recorded use of "OK" [oll korrect] appears in the Boston Morning Post
The ship John Wickliffe arrives at Port Chalmers carrying the first Scottish settlers for Dunedin, New Zealand. Otago province is founded.
Battle of Novara (King Charles Albert vs Italian republic)
18-year-old English chemist William Perkin accidentally produces the first synthetic aniline dye ‘mauveine’ (purple) during his Easter holiday
Streetcar patented (E A Gardner of Philadelphia)
London's 1st tramcars, designed by Mr Train of NY, begins operating
Battle of Kernstown Virginia, Jackson begins his Valley Campaign
Encounter at Camden, Arkansas
University of California founded in Oakland, California
War of the Pacific fought between Chile and the joints forces of Bolivia and Peru. Chile successfully takes over Arica and Tarapacá, leaving Bolivia a landlocked country.
Flour rolling mill patented (John Stevens of Wisconsin)
Boers & Britain sign peace accord; end 1st Boer war
Gas lamp sets fire to Théâtre Municipal” opera house in Nice, France; 70 die, and building completely destroyed
The Edmunds Act (Edmunds Anti-Polygamy Act) is adopted by the US to suppress polygamy. 1300 men are later imprisoned under the act
The free Woolwich Ferry officially opens in east London
The Raines Law is passed by the New York State Legislature, restricting Sunday sale of alcohol to hotels
The Wright brothers 1st file a patent for a flying machine, which is granted 3 years later
American diplomat Durham Stevens is attacked by Korean assassins Jeon Myeong-un and Jang In-hwan, leading to his death in hospital two days later
Zion Mule Corp formed by the British Army
Tornadoes kills 211 over 4 days in Midwest US
Alick Wickham dives 200' into Australia's Yarra River
German forces advance 14 miles to the Somme River (WW1)

Germany begins using long-range gun, the 'Kaiser Wilhelm Geschütz' ('Emperor William Gun'), aka 'Paris Gun' to shell Paris from Crépy-en-Laonnais, 75 miles away; over several days, 303 rounds kill 256 and wound over 600
Lithuania proclaims independence
Bashkir Autonomous Soviet Socialist Republic, designated as the first autonomous republic of Soviet Russia
Perserikatan Communist of India (PKI) political party forms
Germany announces it will be unable to meet its Great War reparation payments
1st airplane lands at the US Capitol in Washington, D.C.
KMJ-AM in Fresno CA begins radio transmissions
WEW-AM in Saint Louis MO begins radio transmissions
Frank Silver and Irving Conn publish their hit song "Yes, We Have No Bananas"
Kroll Opera in Berlin, Germany opens
Italy, Austria & Hungary sign Pact of Rome
LA Railway Co starts using PCC streetcars
First radio broadcast of "Truth or Consequences" on CBS
The Lahore Resolution (Qarardad-e-Lahore), calling for independent Muslim state(s), is adopted by the All-India Muslim League
2,500 Jews of Lublin massacred or deported
Japanese forces occupy Andaman Islands in Indian Ocean
German counterattack on US lines in Tunisia
Italian resistance group bombs occupying German police at Via Rasella, Rome; killing 33 and wounding 110 of the 156 man force; retaliation kills 335 civilians
British 7th Black Watch crosses the Rhine into Germany
British Prime Minister Churchill visits Montgomery's headquarter in Straelen
John Cunningham sets world altitude record (54,492' (18,133 m))
Sidney Kingsley's play "Detective Story" premieres in NYC
Actress Frances Farmer is released from Western State Hospital
Sophocles Venizelos forms liberal Greek government
UN World Meteorological Org established
Wages in France increase 11%
NY Rangers blow 6-2 lead, lose 7-6 to Chicago Black Hawks; Bill Mosienko scores fastest hat trick in NHL history, 21 seconds
Pakistan proclaimed an Islamic republic in Commonwealth (National Day)
Sudan becomes independent
US Army sells its last homing pigeons
NASA's Explorer (8) fails to reach Earth orbit
Nawab of Pataudi captains the Indian cricket team against the West Indies at the age of 21 years and 77 days
Wake Forest coach "Bones" McKinney becomes 2nd person to play and coach
William DeWitt buys Cincinnati Reds for $4,625,000
Rolf Hochhuth's play "Der Stellvertreter" (The Representative) premieres in Berlin
UNCTAD 1 world conference opens in Geneva
Moroccan army shoots on demonstrators, about 100 killed
NASA launches Gemini 3, 1st US 2-man space flight (Gus Grissom & Young)
1st official meeting after 400 years of Catholic & Anglican Church
Rev Walter Fauntroy, is 1st non-voting congressional delegate from DC
Rally for Decency in Miami
US performs nuclear test at Nevada Test Site
Dutch 2nd Chamber accept simplified divorce
USSR performs underground nuclear test
Geoge Harrison and Friends' "The Concert for Bangladesh" concert film, directed by Saul Swimmer released in the US
NY Yankees agree to continue playing ball in the Bronx
After a 5½ year run soap "Love is a Many Splendored Thing" ends
US performs nuclear test at Nevada Test Site
AIAW Women's Basketball Tournament, Immaculata beat Mississippi College 68-53 in Manhattan, Kansas
International Bill of Rights goes into effect (35 nations ratifying)
US performs nuclear test at Nevada Test Site
Wings release "Goodnight Tonight"
AIAW Women's Basketball Tournament, Old Dominion beat Tennessee 68-53 at Mount Pleasant
France performs nuclear test
Nicaraguan National Literary Crusade begins: crusade teachers swear in plazas across the country to exterminate ignorance in just five months (will successfully reduce illiteracy from 50% to 12%)
US Supreme Court rules states could require, with some exceptions, parental notification when teen-age girls sought abortions
US Supreme Court upholds law making statutory rape a crime only for men
Andrea Schone skates ladies world record 3 km (4:20.91)
Ice Pairs Championship at Ottawa won by Underhill & Paul Martini (CAN)
Julian Lennon's 1st concert (San Antonio Texas)
Space Shuttle Discovery moves to Vehicle Assembly Building at Kennedy Space Center (Florida) for mating of STS 51-D mission
US performs nuclear test at Nevada Test Site
Soap opera "The Bold and The Beautiful" premieres on CBS
US offers military protection to Kuwaiti ships in the Persian Gulf
Geffen Records releases "Chalk Mark In A Rain Storm", Canadian singer-songwriterJoni Mitchell's 13th studio album
2 Utah scientists claim they have produced fusion at room temperature
Joel Steinberg sentenced to 25 yrs for killing his adopted daughter
Former Exxon Valdez Captain Joseph Hazelwood ordered to help clean up Prince William Sound & pay $50,000 in restitution for 1989 oil spill
1st World League of American Football games, London beats Frankfurt 24-11, Sacramento beats Raleigh-Dur 9-3 & Mont beats Birmingham 20-5
20 Tornadoes kill 5 in Tennessee
Sergei Bubka pole vaults world record indoor (6.12m)
Florida Marlins begin selling tickets
Belgian government of Jean-Luc Dehaene resigns
NY Knicks & Phoenix Suns get into a major brawl
Graeme Obree bicycles world record 10 km (11:28)

Green Ramp disaster: Mid-air collision of USAF F-16 fighter jet and USAF C-130 Hercules transport kills 24 and injures over 100 US Army paratroopers on the ground preparing for training at Pope AFB in Cumberland County, North Carolina
Richard Jacobs buys naming rights to Indians new ball park at Gateway for $13.8 million (renamed Jacobs Field)
Russian Airbus A-310 crashes in Siberia (74-75 killed)
Dollar equals 88.41 yen (record)
Taiwan holds its first direct elections and chooses Lee Teng-hui as President.
Gunmen assassinate Paraguay's Vice President Luis María Argaña
The Russian Mir space station is disposed of, breaking up in the atmosphere before falling into the southern Pacific Ocean near Fiji
Andhra Pradesh Federation of Trade Unions holds its first conference in Hyderabad, India
Major explosion and fire at the BP's Texas City Refinery kills 15 workers in Texas City, Texas
The United States 11th Circuit Court of Appeals, in a 2-1 decision, refuses to order the reinsertion of Terri Schiavo's feeding tube
The Federal Reserve discontinues publishing M3 money supply
Burnley Tunnel catastrophe occurs in Melbourne, Australia
Iranian Navy seize Royal Navy personnel in Iraqi waters
African Union suspends Mali's membership following a coup
20 people are killed and 200 are injured by a tornado in Brahmanbaria, Bangladesh
The US Senate approves its first budget in four years by a margin of 50–49
Walt Disney Company launches premium cable Disney Junior channel, with 24-hour programming for the 2-7 year old audience
A Sahara sandstorm turns snow in Sochi, Russia, orange in one of the largest-ever transfers of desert sand
Peru's Congress swears in Vice-President Martín Vizcarra as the new President after Pedro Pablo Kuczynski resigned amid corruption scandal
More than 1 million march to demand a new EU referendum in London, England
More than 130 Fulani people killed in Ogossagou, Mali, in attack by Dogon hunters, prompting government ban on the hunters
Syrian Democratic Forces announce that the last Islamic State territory has been retaken, raising flags in Baghuz, Syria, and ending the five-year Islamic State "caliphate"
African American Daniel Prude dies after being physically retrained by police in Rochester, New York, including having a hood put over his head
COVID-19 lockdowns imposed in South Africa and the United Kingdom while other European nations extend and strengthen their lockdowns
New York confirmed as new center of the COVID-19 pandemic in the US with 20,875 cases (5,707 in the last day) and 157 deaths
WHO says the COVID-19 pandemic is accelerating, 1st 100,000 cases took 67 days, 2nd 100,000 cases 11 days, 3rd 100,000 cases 4 days
Cargo ship Ever Given gets stuck in the Suez Canal, Egypt, a 400m megaship it completely blocks the shipping canal
Abel Prize, mathematics' Nobel equivalent, awarded to American Dennis Sullivan for his work on topography
After 114 consecutive weeks as world #1 female tennis player, 25-year-old Australian Ash Barty makes shock retirement announcement; cites contentment with all she achieved in the sport
Brian Houston, Australian founder of megachurch Hillsong, resigns after an internal investigation finds he breached their code of conduct with two women
Canada records record population growth (+2.7%), adding 1 million people in 2022, mostly through immigration
TikTok CEO Shou Zi Chew questioned for over four hours by a US congressional hearing over its Chinese security risks
Utah is the first state to limit social media for minors, including an overnight curfew and parental consents
Conrad IIcrowns himself King of Italy
Treaty of Constance:Frederick IBarbarossa agrees not to reestablish Byzantium in Italy and to support Pope Eugene III
George Frideric Handel's oratorio "Messiah" premieres at the Royal Opera House, Covent Garden, London
Patrick Henryproclaims "Give me liberty or give me death" in a speech encouraging Virginia troops to join the American Revolutionary War
Joseph Haydn's 94th Symphony in G  at the Hanover Square Rooms in London, England
Polish generalTadeusz Kościuszkoreturns to Kraków, Poland to led the Kościuszko Uprising against Russian and Prussian control
Napoleon's brother Joseph takes the throne of Spain
John William Drapertakes 1st successful photo in US of the Moon (daguerreotype), in New York City
Elisha Otisinstalls his first elevator at 488 Broadway in New York City
General Shermanand Cox' troops reach Goldsboro, North Carolina
Congress passes 2nd Reconstruction Act over PresidentAndrew Johnson's veto
Tchaikovsky's "Manfred" symphony premieres in Moscow, Russia, conducted by Max Erdmannsdörfer
Australian opera star DameNellie Melbareveals secret of her now famous toast

8th Congress of the Russian Communist Party re-establishes a five-member Politburo, which becomes the center of political power in the Soviet Union. Original members areVladimir Lenin,Leon Trotsky,Joseph Stalin, Lev Kamenev, and Nikolai Krestinsky
Benito Mussoliniforms Fascist group Fasci di Combattimento (Italian Fighting Bands) in Milan, Italy - later reorganized into National Fascist Party
Indian independence fightersBhagat Singh, Shivaram Rajguru and Sukhdev Thapar are hanged after conducting an assassination and a bombing. Their request to be shot by a firing squad is refused.
German Reichstag hastily passes the Enabling Act and PresidentPaul von Hindenburgsigns it the same day, grantingAdolf Hitlerdictatorial powers
PhysicianJoseph G. Hamiltoninjects a leukemia patient with a sodium radioisotope, first intravenous injection of a human with a radioisotope
MLB CommissionerKenesaw Mountain Landisfrees 74 St Louis Cardinals minor league players from their contracts
1st performance ofBéla Bartók's 2nd Violin Concerto at the Concertgebow, Amsterdam with Zoltán Székely on violin and Willem Mengelberg conducting the Concertgebouw Orchestra
Lt. Gen.Miles Dempseybecomes the first British commander to cross the Rhine during the Allied invasion of Germany
The Swallow Sidecar Company headed byWilliam Lyonsagrees to change its name to Jaguar
22nd Academy Awards: "All The King's Men",Broderick CrawfordandOlivia De Havillandwin
JFKvisits San Francisco
"In His Own Write", a book of short stories, poems and drawings byJohn Lennonis published
Reprise Records releases "Song To A Seagull", Canadian singer-songwriterJoni Mitchell's debut studio album
"Hello Darlin'" single released byConway Twitty(Billboard Song of the Year, 1970)
Yoko Onois granted permanent residence in USA
Elvis Presleybegins his final concert tour
Larry HolmesTKOs Osvaldo Ocasio in 7 for heavyweight boxing title
18-year old American tennis starTracy Austinwins her only WTA Tour Championship; upsetsMartina Navratilovain the final 6–2, 2–6, 6–2 in NYC
Australian cricketerAllan Borderbecomes the first and only batsman to reach 150 in each innings of a test, in the 3rd test vs Pakistan in Lahore
Guatemala military coup under GeneralEfraín Ríos Montt, President Romeo Lucas flees
NHL New York IslandersMike Bossy's 20th career hat trick, in his 5th career 4-goal game, an 8-1 win over visiting Washington Capitols
US PresidentRonald Reaganintroduces Strategic Defense Initiative ("Star Wars")
World Ice Dancing Championship won byJayne TorvillandChristopher Dean(Great Britain) in Ottawa, Canada

David Bowieis special surprise guest atTina Turnerconcert at NEC arena in Birmingham, England; they perform duets of Bowie's "Tonight" and a medley of Chris Montez' "Let's Dance" and Bowie's song of the same name
First of 2 WTA Tour Championships of the year; change of tennis schedule;Martina Navratilovawins 4th straight and 7th overall title 6–2, 6–0, 3–6, 6–1 against Hana Mandlíková in NYC
Former West German ChancellorWilly Brandtresigns as chairman of the Social Democratic Party
Amy Fisher's lover Joey Buttafuoco is released from jail after 4 months & 9 days
Howard Sternformally announces his Libertarian run for NY governor
Wayne Gretzkysets NHL record with 802 goals scored
70th Academy Awards: "Titanic",Jack Nicholson&Helen Huntwin
"Livin' la Vida Loca" sung byRicky Martinreleased - goes on to sell over 8 million copies
Joe Sakicrecords his 400th career goal and becomes the Quebec Nordiques/Colorado Avalanche all-time leading point scorer
75th Academy Awards: "Chicago" (Best Picture),Adrien Brody(Best Actor - "The Piano") &Nicole Kidman(Best Actress - "The Hours") win
ICC Men's Cricket ODI World Cup, Wanderers Stadium, Johannesburg, South Africa: Australia retain trophy with 125 run win over India; Player of the Match:Ricky Ponting(AUS) 140 no (121)
Usherreleases his 4th studio album "Confessions" (2004 Billboard Album of the Year, Grammy Award Best Contemporary R&B Album 2005)
After some initial hesitation,Emma Watsonsigns on to appear as Hermione in the final three Harry Potter films
US PresidentBarack Obamasigns the Affordable Care Act (ACA), nicknamed 'Obamacare', expanding the availability and affordability of health care insurance
Chris Sharmabecomes the second person to climb a 9b+ (5.15c) route with his ascent of La Dura Dura in Oliana, Spain
British entertainerJames Cordenbecomes the host of "The Late Late Show" on CBS; hosts the program through 2023
GPR investigation ofShakespeare's tomb at Holy Trinity Church in Stratford concludes the Bard's skull probably has been stolen
Italy signs up to China's Belt and Road Initiative with deals signed in Rome between Italian Deputy Prime Minister Luigi Di Maio and Chinese PresidentXi Jinping
Brazil's daily COVID-19 death toll passes 3,000 (3,251) for the first time (298,676 total deaths reported) as country's Supreme Court blocks PresidentJair Bolsonarooverturning local restrictions
First ever tweet by Twitter founderJack Dorsey"just setting up my twttr" sells for $2.9 million in digitally autographed version
US Secretary of StateAntony Blinkenformally declares that members of Russia armed forces have committed war crimes in Ukraine
Harry KaneovertakesWayne Rooney's record to become England's all-time greatest goalscorer in 2-1 victory over Italy with his 54th goal
Lewis Koumasmakes his debut for the Wales U19 national team against Scotland U19
Aleksandr Pavlovich Romanov becomesTsar Alexander Iof Russia after his father Tsar Paul I is assassinated
Battle of Okinawa: US Navy ships bomb the Japanese island of Okinawa in preparation for the Allied invasion; the largest battle of the Pacific War in World War II
In Nasiriyah, Iraq, 11 soldiers of the 507th Maintenance Company as well as 18 U.S. Marines are killed during the first major conflict of Operation Iraqi Freedom
Go-Kōgon, 4th Japanese Emperor of the Northern Court (1352-71), born in Japan (d. 1374)
Thomas Selle, German baroque composer, born in Zörbig, Electorate of Saxony, Holy Roman Empire (d. 1663)
